name: Fort Gordon
tagline: The Army's cyber and signal headquarters near Augusta, Georgia, home of Army Cyber Command.
_note: Army cyber/signal hub (Augusta GA; Eastern time). CONUS full template (BAH MHA GA073 Fort Gordon; RentCast/SchoolDigger work). NAME HISTORY: Fort Gordon -> Fort Eisenhower (Oct 2023) -> Fort Gordon (2025; effective June 11, 2025, formally Sept 26, 2025). Now honors MSG Gary I. Gordon (Medal of Honor, Battle of Mogadishu/Black Hawk Down), NOT the Confederate general (verified via Army.mil + WRDW + Wikipedia, June 2026). Home of Army Cyber Center of Excellence, ARCYBER HQ, NSA Georgia, Signal Corps. Medical: Dwight D. Eisenhower Army Medical Center (DDEAMC; hospital named for Eisenhower since 1974, predates base rename). Georgia is TRICARE East. Medium-confidence flags: base coords (approx), off-base ZIP 30809 (Evans).

data.Role: The Army's cyber and signal headquarters
data.Major commands: Cyber Center of Excellence, Army Cyber Command (ARCYBER), and NSA Georgia
data.Current name: Fort Gordon (now honoring MSG Gary I. Gordon, Medal of Honor)
data.Name note: Was Fort Eisenhower from 2023 to 2025 before reverting

sections.title: Mission & Identity
data: Fort Gordon is the Army's center for cyber and signal operations, home to the Cyber Center of Excellence, the headquarters of Army Cyber Command, and a major National Security Agency presence (NSA Georgia). It's a high-tech, fast-growing hub for the Army's information and cyber forces.

Near Augusta, Georgia, in the Central Savannah River Area, the post carried the name Fort Eisenhower from 2023 to 2025 before reverting to Fort Gordon, now honoring a Medal of Honor recipient rather than the Confederate general.

sections.title: Augusta Living: Affordable, With Strong School Options

data.subtitle: The Augusta area is affordable, and the key choice for many families is Columbia County (popular schools) versus closer-in options, balanced against your commute.
intro: Augusta's cost of living is reasonable, so BAH stretches, and the cyber mission draws a lot of families and a growing tech community. On-post housing is available; many live off post.
Where you live mostly comes down to commute and schools, with Columbia County a favorite of families for its schools, and closer-in options trading a shorter drive for a different school mix.

recommendations.advice: Columbia County (Evans, Grovetown, Martinez) is the favorite for families, with well-regarded schools a manageable drive out.
Areas near the post and into Augusta (Richmond County) keep the commute short.
On-post housing is available and convenient; weigh it against the affordable local market.
Augusta is affordable, so BAH tends to go far and many families buy.

sections.title: Gates & Access
data: Visitors without a DoD ID need a visitor pass and a REAL ID-compliant ID from the Visitor Control Center.
Gate hours and visitor requirements change, so confirm the current details on the official Fort Gordon site before you go.

sections.title: On-Base Housing
data: Fort Gordon has privatized on-post family housing across several neighborhoods.
Eligibility and availability vary by rank and family size, so start with the housing office for the current manager, options, and waitlists.

source.label: DoD 2026 BAH Rates, MHA GA073 (Fort Gordon)

sections.title: Schools
data: There is no DoDEA district here; children attend the local civilian public schools. Columbia County (Evans, Grovetown, Martinez) is highly regarded and popular with families; Richmond County serves Augusta and the area closest to the post.
Your assigned schools depend on your address, so confirm the district and campus for any neighborhood you are considering.

data.Hospital: Dwight D. Eisenhower Army Medical Center (DDEAMC)
data.Care: A full Army medical center with primary, specialty, inpatient, and emergency care, plus graduate medical education

intro: Whether to rent or buy is the biggest money decision of most PCS moves, and Augusta's affordable, growing market makes buying a real option for many families.
rent: Your tour is short (about two years or less) or your orders feel uncertain.
You want time to learn the area and pick the right county for schools.
You'd rather not gamble on resale timing when you PCS out.
Your BAH comfortably covers rent in the areas you'd consider.
buy: You expect to be here three or more years, common with the cyber mission.
You want to use your VA loan and build equity instead of paying rent.
The affordable, growing Augusta market fits your budget for a home you like.
You've found a neighborhood and commute you're confident about.
data.bottom_line: There is no universal right answer, but Augusta's affordability and growth make buying worth a look for longer tours. Run your BAH against the rent and home prices above, weigh your tour length, and talk to a lender and an agent who work with military families before you commit.

intro: The first month at a new duty station is mostly logistics. Knocking these out early makes everything else easier.

steps.title: Line up temporary lodging
steps.text: Book on-post lodging or a short-term rental before you arrive. You can claim Temporary Lodging Expense (TLE) for a limited number of days while you house-hunt.

steps.title: In-process and update DEERS
steps.text: Report to your unit and finance, and make sure DEERS and ID cards are current for every family member so TRICARE and base access work.

steps.title: Start with the Housing Office
steps.text: Get the housing office's read on Columbia County (schools) versus closer-in options before you sign anything.

steps.title: Register your vehicle and sort access
steps.text: Handle base access and Georgia vehicle registration, and learn the visitor-pass process for guests. Bring your registration and insurance.

steps.title: Enroll the kids in school
steps.text: Contact the gaining district (Columbia or Richmond County) early and bring records and immunizations.

steps.title: Set up healthcare
steps.text: Update your TRICARE enrollment so you can be seen at Eisenhower Army Medical Center or network providers.

data.q: Is it Fort Gordon or Fort Eisenhower?
data.a: Fort Gordon, as of 2025. It was Fort Eisenhower from 2023 to 2025; the name now honors Master Sergeant Gary I. Gordon, a Medal of Honor recipient from the 1993 Battle of Mogadishu, rather than the Confederate general.

data.q: What does the Army do at Fort Gordon?
data.a: It's the Army's cyber and signal headquarters, home to the Cyber Center of Excellence, Army Cyber Command, and a major NSA presence.
On-post housing is available; many live off post. Columbia County (Evans, Grovetown, Martinez) is the favorite for schools; Richmond County is closest to the post.
Fort Gordon is MHA GA073. Use the calculator above for your grade.
